Company Description

NVM & Associates, led by five experienced Chartered Accountant partners, specializes in providing expert guidance to businesses in India by managing their compliance requirements, allowing them to focus on growth and operations. The firm is recognized for its robust work culture, smart professional team, and efficient service delivery. NVM & Associates operates in key areas such as taxation (Income Tax, GST), audit and assurance, management consultancy, business setup and structuring, financial strategy, and internal audits and controls. Their proven methodologies and commitment to excellence make them a trusted partner for businesses.

Role Description

The CA - Ops Head will oversee the operational functions of the firm, ensuring streamlined processes, team management, and client satisfaction. Responsibilities include strategizing and monitoring internal operations, managing budgets, analyzing business operations, and ensuring regulatory compliance. Additionally, the role will require fostering an efficient and supportive work environment, focusing on developing and mentoring team members. This is a full-time, on-site role located in the Pune/Pimpri-Chinchwad Area.

Qualifications

• Proficiency in Operations Management and Budgeting to efficiently oversee and allocate resources and execute operational strategies.
• Strong Analytical Skills to interpret financial data, identify trends, and make performance-driven decisions.
• Experience in Team Management and Communication to lead, mentor, and collaborate with diverse teams effectively.
• In-depth knowledge of compliance, management consultancy, taxation, and financial strategic planning.
• Chartered Accountant qualification is mandatory. Experience in a leadership or operations-focused role is highly preferred.
• Ability to work efficiently on-site in Pune/Pimpri-Chinchwad Area with a focus on delivering timely and high-quality outcomes.

data.job_title: Senior Accountant - Lease Accounting and Financial Reporting
data.job_description: Senior Accountant - Lease The Opportunity: Assist in the preparation of external and internal financial reporting including all periodic Securities & Exchange Commission (“SEC”) filings. Work closely with different functions including Corporate Consolidations, Global Finance, Legal, Tax, Treasury and internal and external auditors. Facilitate the information flow from all subsidiaries and the process of preparing SEC filings. Assist with improvements to financial reporting processes. This role is responsible for supporting core corporate accounting activities, including lease accounting under ASC 842, stock‑based compensation accounting under ASC 718, monthly close processes, and coordination with global teams. The position requires strong analytical abilities, attention to detail, and effective cross‑functional collaboration. Major Responsibilities Lease Accounting & Fixed Assets Enter new leases and update lease changes in the Lease Query Tool. Prepare and record monthly journal entries and payment reconciliations in alignment with ASC 842. Collaborate with global teams to support centralized lease accounting processes. Stock‑Based Compensation Prepare and analyze stock‑based compensation expenses in accordance with ASC 718. Support quarterly disclosure requirements for 10Q/10K filings. Month-End Close & Reporting Support the monthly close process, including preparation and review of journal entries, account reconciliations, analytical reviews, and documentation. Prepare monthly results for reporting into the data mapping tool for consolidated financials. Assist Financial Reporting and Consolidation teams with close activities and data needs. Business Partnership & Collaboration Build strong working relationships with finance leaders and cross‑functional teams. Develop a solid understanding of financial processes, inter‑department data flows, reporting needs, and ongoing initiatives. Communicate insights clearly and responsibly to the Controller’s Group. Audit Support Support quarterly internal and external audit processes with accurate, compliant documentation. Process Improvement Continuously evaluate existing processes to identify opportunities to improve efficiency, accessibility of financial data, and data integrity. Ad‑hoc Support Perform ad‑hoc financial and economic analyses as requested. Contribute to special projects and other duties as assigned. Qualifications Education & Experience CA Intermediate with 3–6 years of relevant experience, or CA with 1–2 years of experience in Record to Report Demonstrated experience with technical accounting topics such as Lease Accounting and Stock‑Based Compensation. Proficiency in Microsoft Excel required; experience with SAP is mandatory. Knowledge, Skills & Abilities Strong understanding of U.S. GAAP, IFRS, and corporate accounting policies. Familiarity with Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) requirements, with experience applying relevant controls. Ability to prepare, interpret, and analyze financial statements, including P&L, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow. Strong written and verbal communication skills to effectively collaborate with multiple teams and stakeholders. Strong analytical mindset, organizational skills, and the ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks. Proactive, detail‑oriented, and committed to continuous learning. They are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of employees assigned to this position. Company Description

Techdome is a technology-driven company specializing in developing innovative solutions to help clients achieve differentiation and success. Backed by a team of skilled technical and domain experts, we explore all possibilities to drive impactful development. Our work empowers transformative companies and influencers across diverse industries, including aviation, manufacturing, finance, insurance, and hospitality.

From startups to Fortune 500 companies, Techdome delivers solutions that make a difference.

Role Description

As a Corporate Finance Associate, you will play a pivotal role in financial planning and analysis, building financial models, conducting due diligence, and evaluating financial performance to support business decisions. This is a full-time position based on-site in Hyderabad, where teamwork and collaboration drive innovation and success.

We are seeking a skilled Corporate Finance Associate to manage company financials, oversee loan granting processes, and ensure compliance with financial standards across Indian, USA, and UAE markets**.

The ideal candidate has a strong accounting background or is pursuing CA certification, and is comfortable working across multiple jurisdictions in a fast-moving, AI-driven finance environment.

This role sits within Techdome's Finance Wizard practice — our AI-driven finance function — and is an internal, full-time position.

Qualifications

• Strong Analytical Skills to interpret complex data and provide actionable insights
• Proficiency in Financial Modeling, including projection modeling and valuation principles
• Comprehensive understanding of Finance and Financial Analysis to support decision-making
• Experience with performing meticulous Due Diligence for assessing investments, acquisitions, or transactions
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field
• Excellent communication and presentation skills to convey financial insights effectively
• Proficiency in financial software such as Excel, ERP systems, and other relevant analytical tools
• Prior experience in corporate finance, investment banking, or consulting is an asset

Responsibilities

Financial Management

• Manage company financials, including bookkeeping, month-end close, and financial reporting
• Prepare and maintain accurate books of accounts, ledgers, and reconciliations
• Support preparation of financial statements (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow)
• Track receivables, payables, and working capital
• Prepare management reports and financial dashboards

Loan Granting & Processing

• Oversee the end-to-end loan granting process
• Review loan applications and supporting financial documentation
• Support credit assessment, underwriting, and due diligence
• Prepare loan documentation, sanction notes, and disbursement records
• Monitor repayments, interest accruals, and borrower compliance
• Maintain loan registers and related MIS

Compliance — India / USA / UAE

• Ensure compliance with applicable financial standards and regulatory requirements across Indian, USA, and UAE markets

- India: GST, TDS, income tax, ROC filings, Companies Act compliance

- USA: Federal and state tax filings, 1099/W-9 handling, applicable sales tax

- UAE: VAT returns, corporate tax filings, relevant statutory requirements

• Maintain a compliance calendar and ensure timely filings across jurisdictions
• Coordinate with external consultants, CAs, and auditors

Analytical Support

• Analyze financial data to identify trends, variances, and risks
• Support budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis
• Provide analytical inputs for management decision-making

Audit & Internal Controls

• Support statutory, tax, and internal audits
• Maintain documentation, audit schedules, and reconciliations
• Strengthen internal financial controls and SOPs

Preferred Qualifications

• Multi-entity or multi-country finance exposure
• Experience in a CA firm, consulting firm, or financial services organization
• Familiarity with AI tools and automation in finance workflows

Required Skills

• Accounting
• Financial Management
• Loan Processing
• Knowledge of Indian, USA, and UAE markets
• Compliance
• Analytical Skills

data.job_description:
FP&A Senior Associate - Revenue Reporting The Apex Group was established in Bermuda in 2003 and is now one of the world’s largest fund administration and middle office solutions providers. Our business is unique in its ability to reach globally, service locally and provide cross-jurisdictional services. With our clients at the heart of everything we do, our hard-working team has successfully delivered on an unprecedented growth and transformation journey, and we are now represented by over circa 13,000 employees across 112 offices worldwide.Your career with us should reflect your energy and passion.

That’s why, at Apex Group, we will do more than simply ‘empower’ you. We will work to supercharge your unique skills and experience. Take the lead and we’ll give you the support you need to be at the top of your game.

And we offer you the freedom to be a positive disrupter and turn big ideas into bold, industry-changing realities. For our business, for clients, and for you About the Company Apex Group Ltd., established in Bermuda in 2003 is one of the world’s largest financial service providers with c.13,000 employees operating out of c.85 offices in 42 countries. The Group has c.$3 trillion assets under management and has delivered strong growth of >10% year on year for the last 3 years.

Apex Group offers a single source solution to customers for financial services as it has the broadest range of services amongst its competitors. These services include fund administration solutions, digital on-boarding of bank accounts, depositary, custody and super ManCo services, corporate and family office services, business services including HR and payroll and a pioneering ESG ratings and advisory service for private companies.

Pune Global Service

Centre FP&A Team India is growing as an Apex Group finance hub as it provides intelligent, enthusiastic, and energetic professionals to compliment a strong global finance team. This is a great opportunity to join the Global FP&A Team who have chosen Pune as their FP&A Global Service Centre (GSC). The FP&A Pune Team started with a 7-member team in 2021 in Phase 1.

Following early successes of the Pune team and the need for increased reporting to stakeholders following a high number of recent business acquisitions, the team grew to 32 members by 2024 in the subsequent hiring phases. We now have a team of technically strong and energetic employees and middle management professionals delivering an increasing number of data sets, standardised deliverables, and improved analytical reporting to assist the Global FP&A team in managing reporting for the entire portfolio of Group Companies. The FP&A organisational structure is relatively flat thereby increasing opportunities for everyone to grow and build a successful career.

We are looking to hire team members who will complement our world class high performing team. These roles are expected to drive data cleansing, standardised processing and be engaging with business, management, and finance teams across the globe.

The Role

This is an exciting opportunity for a Finance professional to join a high performing Global FP&A team operating in a fast growth Global Financial Services organisation. We have a few openings at Associate, Senior Associate, Assistant Vice President and Vice President and Senior Vice President Levels. The Global FP&A team is functionally structured with Group Reporting (which includes synergy reporting as well), Revenue, People Cost and Other Expense Reporting and Partnering as the main pillars who also play a major role in stake holder partnership and management.

The Entire

Team and each Pillar is led by extremely knowledgeable, experienced, dedicated, and professional leaders. data.job_description: MS Dynamics-Tech Risk-Senior At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence. We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. Join EY and help to build a better working world. Job Description At EY, we offer a unique opportunity to build a career that reflects your individuality, supported by a global network, an inclusive culture, and cutting-edge technology. Your unique voice and perspective are vital to our mission of continuously improving EY. Join us to create an exceptional experience for yourself and contribute to a better working world for everyone. EY - Technology Risk – MS D365 Senior As a key member of our EY Technology Risk team, you will technically contribute to IT Risk and Assurance client engagements and internal projects. A crucial aspect of your role includes actively developing and maintaining both internal and external relationships. You will have the opportunity to identify potential business opportunities within existing engagements and escalate these appropriately. Moreover, you'll be responsible for anticipating and identifying risks within engagements and communicating any issues to senior members of the team. The Opportunity We are seeking candidates at the Senior level to expand the leadership group of our EY Technology Risk Team. This role offers a remarkable chance to be part of a leading firm and play a significant role in the development of our new service offerings, particularly in the MS D365 domain. Your Key Responsibilities Engage in IT Risk and Assurance engagements with a focus on MS D365 technologies. Collaborate effectively as a team member, sharing responsibilities, providing support, maintaining communication, and updating senior team members on progress. Assist in the preparation of audit reports for clients and other stakeholders. Develop and maintain productive relationships with onshore teams and client personnel. Conduct identification and testing of IT security and IT risk (e.g., data systems, network, and applications) within MS D365 environments. Contribute to practice-wide training initiatives focused on MS D365 IT General Controls (ITGC), Security, and Pre & Post Implementation reviews. Work closely with onshore, cross-functional teams to develop strong project senior relationships across the organization. Stay updated with the latest MS D365 ERP versions and functionalities, promoting industry best practices. Actively participate in project management and stakeholder management, including planning and budget preparation. Skills and Attributes for Success Experience in reviewing and testing IT general controls (ITGC) within Microsoft Dynamics AX and Microsoft D365 environments, specifically focusing on access management, change management, computer operations, SOD and SDLC. Proficiency in reviewing and testing Microsoft Dynamics AX and Microsoft D365 security configurations. Ability to perform pre & post-implementation reviews in Microsoft Dynamics AX and Microsoft D365 environments, including migration testing (preferably from Microsoft Dynamics to Microsoft D365). Familiarity with data extraction methods and user access security architecture. Experience with integration capabilities and testing of interface controls between systems. Experience in reviewing and testing the key business process configurations (ITAC’s) Knowledge of auditing methodologies, financial audit IT audits, related regulations, auditing standards, and guidelines. Experience in performing the walkthrough (Test of design) directly with the client, Operating Effectiveness and have knowledge of the financial statement’s assertions. Understanding of control frameworks (e.g., COSO) and common IT governance, control, and assurance industry frameworks (e.g., COBIT, ISACA). Experience in reviewing and testing the key reports ensuring the risks (completeness & accuracy) related to IPE’s (Information Produced by Entity) are addressed. Experience in testing of interface controls between multiple systems and middleware controls. Experience in IT audit in the context of a financial audit & related regulations, auditing standards and guidelines. Awareness of third-party attestation standards and other reporting and industry-specific standards. Qualifications B.E/B.Tech in CS/IT or MBA (preferably in Information Systems) or CA with at least 3 years of experience. MS D365 functional modules certification (Preferred). CISA certification (Preferred). ISO 27001:2013 certification (Preferred). Any other relevant IT audit or security certification (Preferred). What We Look For A Team of people with commercial acumen, technical experience, and enthusiasm to learn new things in this fast-moving environment. Are you ready to trade your job for a journey? Become a FlyMate!

Passion, excitement & global collaboration are all core to what it means to be a FlyMate. At Flywire, we’re on a mission to deliver the world’s most important and complex payments. We use our Flywire Advantage - the combination of our next-gen payments platform, proprietary payment network and vertical specific software, to help our clients get paid, and help their customers pay with ease - no matter where they are in the world.

What more do we need to truly be unstoppable? Perhaps, that is you!

Who we are:

Flywire is a global payments enablement and software company, founded more than a decade ago to solve high-stakes, high-value payments in higher education. We’ve since scaled into new regions and industry verticals and expanded our product offerings to deliver meaningful value to our clients around the world.

Today we support more than 4,900+ clients across the global education, healthcare, travel & B2B industries, with diverse payment methods across 240 countries & territories and more than 140 currencies.

With over 1,200 global FlyMates, representing more than 40 nationalities, and in 12 offices world-wide, we’re looking for FlyMates to join the next stage of our journey as we continue to grow.a

Job Description

At Flywire, we are hiring our first Credit Risk Manager based in Bangalore or Pune. In this role, you will be a key member of the credit risk team, leading the tactical execution of credit policies, managing a portfolio of accounts, and leveraging data-driven insights to safeguard the company’s financial health.

We are seeking a versatile professional who can analyze complex financial statements in depth, partner with Analytics on model automation initiatives, and effectively manage case queues—while balancing multiple priorities in a dynamic environment.

What You’ll Do:

• Execute the Strategy: Implement and refine the global credit risk framework across our key verticals (Education, Healthcare, Travel, and B2B), ensuring localized and vertical nuances are captured and addressed.
• Lead the Underwriting Engine: Manage the end-to-end credit assessment process for new and existing clients. You will be the final touchpoint for complex deals, ensuring deep due diligence and a balanced approach to risk and growth.
• Advanced Analytics & Modeling: Be a "hands-on" contributor to our risk infrastructure. You will leverage SQL and Python to analyze payment trends, validate credit scoring models, and improve the accuracy of our risk decisioning.
• Continuous Account Monitoring: Own the lifecycle of the merchant relationship. You will architect and manage real-time account monitoring protocols, tracking processing and settlement patterns and financial health markers to ensure our exposure remains within appetite.
• Portfolio Health & Early Warning: Actively monitor the global portfolio for early warning signals of deteriorating credit. When a signal is triggered, you will lead the immediate investigation and propose remediation or collateralization strategies.

Qualifications

Here's what we are looking for:

• The Experienced Analyst: You bring a deep understanding of credit risk, specifically within Payments, Fintech, or Banking. You understand merchant credit, settlement risk, and the complexities of global fund flows.
• Educational & Professional Foundation: * A Master’s Degree in Engineering, Economics, or a related quantitative field with 5+ years of relevant experience.
• OR a Bachelor’s Degree with 8+ years of relevant experience in a credit risk or underwriting leadership role.
• Technically Proficient: You are highly proficient with data. You have advanced skills in SQL or Python and are comfortable using BI tools (Tableau, Looker) to visualize portfolio risk.
• The Decision Maker: You are comfortable making high-stakes decisions in a fast-paced environment.
• Collaborative Communicator: You can translate complex credit concepts into actionable insights for Sales and Product teams, acting as a key partner.

Additional Information

What We Offer:

• Competitive compensation
• Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
• Flying Start - Our immersive Global Induction Program (Meet our Execs & Global Teams)
• Work with brilliant people that will keep you on your toes, learn more about their journeys by checking out #InsideFlywire on social media
• Dynamic & Global Team (we have been collaborating virtually for years!)
• Wellbeing Programs (Mental Health, Wellness, Yoga/Pilates/HIIT Classes) with Global FlyMates
• Be a meaningful part in our success - every FlyMate makes an impact
• Competitive time off including FlyBetter Days to volunteer in a cause you believe in and
• Digital Disconnect Days!
• Great Talent & Development Programs (Managers Taking Flight – for new or aspiring managers!)
Submit today and get started!

We are excited to get to know you! Throughout our process you can expect to meet with different FlyMates including the Hiring Manager, Peers on the team, the VP of the department, and a skills assessment. Your Talent Acquisition Partner will walk you through the steps and be your “go-to” person for any questions.

Flywire is an equal opportunity employer. With over 40 nationalities across 14 different offices diversity, gender equality, and inclusion are at the core of our people agenda. We believe our FlyMates are our greatest asset, and we’re excited to watch our unique culture evolve with each new hire.
